datasets.name: 20newsW100
datasets.description: Nodes represent news articles. Each hyperedge represents a word and contains the set of articles in which that word occurs. A dataset from the UCI Categorical Machine Learning Repository. The dataset contains 16,242 articles with binary occurrence values of 100 words. Each word is regarded as a hyperedge and the news articles are vertices. Dataset construction following Yang et al. (Chaoqi Yang, Ruijie Wang, Shuochao Yao, and Tarek Abdelzaher. Hypergraph learning with line expansion. arXiv preprint arXiv:2005.04843, 2020.).

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: DAWN
datasets.description: The Drug Abuse Warning Network (DAWN) temporal higher-order network. Nodes are drugs, and each timestamped hyperedge is the set of drugs reported by a patient during an emergency department visit. Timestamps are recorded at quarter-year resolution from 2004 to 2011.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., DAWN).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., true).

edges_metadata.original_timestamp: (INT) Quarter-year timestamp encoded as year * 4 + quarter (e.g., 8017).
edges_metadata.year: (INT) Calendar year of the emergency department visit (e.g., 2004).
edges_metadata.quarter: (INT) Calendar quarter of the emergency department visit, from 1 to 4 (e.g., 1).
edges_metadata.time: (INT) Normalized quarter-year timestamp used by the temporal hypergraph (e.g., 0).
edges_metadata.weight: (INT) Number of identical visits collapsed into this timestamped hyperedge (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: ECO-M_PL_015_plants
datasets.description: Nodes represent plant species. Each hyperedge represents a pollinator and contains the set of plant species visited by that pollinator (projection of a plant–pollinator bipartite network). This dataset contains the relationships between plants and pollinators from the 'Web of Life' project.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.name: (STRING) Scientific name of the plant species (e.g., Taraxacum officinale).
nodes_metadata.original_node_id: (INT) Original node identifier from the source dataset (e.g., 667).

edges_metadata.name: (STRING) Scientific name of the pollinator species (e.g., Eristalis tenax).

datasets.name: Marvel
datasets.description: A hypergraph representing comic book characters as nodes and the comic books they co-appear in as hyperedges. This dataset combines information from the DC-MARVEL Comic Characters Dataset and the Marvel Universe Social Network Dataset to create a comprehensive representation of character interactions within the Marvel universe. Not all characters have complete information.
datasets.source: GigaSheet: DC-MARVEL Comic Characters Dataset (https://www.gigasheet.com/sample-data/dc-marvel-comic-characters) and Marvel Universe Social Network Dataset (https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/csanhueza/the-marvel-universe-social-network?resource=download&select=edges.csv)

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., Marvel).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.Alive: (STRING) Whether the character is currently alive (e.g., Yes).
nodes_metadata.Eyes: (STRING) Eye color of the character (e.g., Red).
nodes_metadata.FirstAppearance: (STRING) Date of first comic book appearance (year, month; e.g., 1977, July).
nodes_metadata.Hair: (STRING) Hair color of the character (e.g., Black).
nodes_metadata.Identity: (STRING) Public or secret identity status of the character (e.g., Known to Authorities).
nodes_metadata.Planet: (STRING) Planet or universe of origin (e.g., Earth-616).
nodes_metadata.Sex: (STRING) Sex or gender category recorded for the character (e.g., Male).

edges_metadata.comic: (STRING) Comic issue or publication code represented by the hyperedge (e.g., MM 12).

datasets.name: ModelNet40
datasets.description: The Princeton ModelNet40 dataset is a benchmark dataset from the area of computer graphics first introduced by Wu et al. (Zhirong Wu, Shuran Song, Aditya Khosla, Fisher Yu, Linguang Zhang, Xiaoou Tang, and Jianxiong Xiao. 3d shapenets: A deep representation for volumetric shapes. In Proceedings of the IEEE conference on computer vision and pattern recognition, pp. 1912–1920, 2015.) The ModelNet40 dataset consists of 12,311 objects from 40 popular categories and the graph structure is constructed using a probability graph based on node distance. See 'Hypergraph Neural Networks' (https://arxiv.org/pdf/1809.09401) for details on construction.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: Mushroom
datasets.description: The Mushroom dataset includes descriptions of hypothetical samples corresponding to 23 species of gilled mushrooms in the Agaricus and Lepiota Family. Each node corresponds to an instance of a mushroom and hyperedges are constructed according to co-occurrences of specific attribute values. For example, all mushrooms with a brown cap form a hyperedge. There are 8124 instances (nodes) described by 22 categorical attributes, leading to a total of 272 hyperedges. Construction following the methodology used by the authors of 'YOU ARE ALLSET: A MULTISET LEARNING FRAME-WORK FOR HYPERGRAPH NEURAL NETWORKS'.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.bruises: (STRING) Whether the mushroom shows bruises (t=bruises, f=no bruises) (e.g., t).
nodes_metadata.cap-color: (STRING) Cap color code (b=buff, c=cinnamon, e=red, g=gray, n=brown, p=pink, r=green, u=purple, w=white, y=yellow) (e.g., n).
nodes_metadata.cap-shape: (STRING) Cap shape code (b=bell, c=conical, f=flat, k=knobbed, s=sunken, x=convex) (e.g., x).
nodes_metadata.cap-surface: (STRING) Cap surface texture code (f=fibrous, g=grooves, s=smooth, y=scaly) (e.g., s).
nodes_metadata.gill-attachment: (STRING) Gill attachment type code (a=attached, d=descending, f=free, n=notched) (e.g., f).
nodes_metadata.gill-color: (STRING) Gill color code (same encoding as cap-color) (e.g., k).
nodes_metadata.gill-spacing: (STRING) Gill spacing code (c=close, w=crowded, d=distant) (e.g., c).
nodes_metadata.habitat: (STRING) Habitat type code (d=woods, g=grasses, l=leaves, m=meadows, p=paths, u=urban, w=waste) (e.g., u).
nodes_metadata.odor: (STRING) Odor type code (a=almond, c=creosote, f=foul, l=anise, m=musty, n=none, p=pungent, s=spicy, y=fishy) (e.g., p).
nodes_metadata.population: (STRING) Population pattern code (a=abundant, c=clustered, n=numerous, s=scattered, v=several, y=solitary) (e.g., s).
nodes_metadata.ring-number: (STRING) Number of rings code (n=none, o=one, t=two) (e.g., o).
nodes_metadata.ring-type: (STRING) Ring type code (c=cobwebby, e=evanescent, f=flaring, l=large, n=none, p=pendant, s=sheathing, z=zone) (e.g., p).
nodes_metadata.spore-print-color: (STRING) Spore print color code (same encoding as cap-color) (e.g., k).
nodes_metadata.stalk-color-above-ring: (STRING) Stalk color above ring code (same encoding as cap-color) (e.g., w).
nodes_metadata.stalk-color-below-ring: (STRING) Stalk color below ring code (same encoding as cap-color) (e.g., w).
nodes_metadata.stalk-surface-above-ring: (STRING) Stalk surface texture above ring code (f=fibrous, k=silky, s=smooth, y=scaly) (e.g., s).
nodes_metadata.stalk-surface-below-ring: (STRING) Stalk surface texture below ring code (f=fibrous, k=silky, s=smooth, y=scaly) (e.g., s).
nodes_metadata.veil-color: (STRING) Veil color code (same encoding as cap-color) (e.g., w).

edges_metadata.cap-shape|cap-surface|cap-color|bruises: (LIST[STRING]) Attribute-value tuple defining the hyperedge for cap shape, cap surface, cap color, and bruises (e.g., [b, f, g, f]).
edges_metadata.odor|gill-attachment|gill-spacing|gill-s: (LIST[STRING]) Attribute-value tuple defining the hyperedge for odor and gill attributes (e.g., [a, f, c, b, n]).
edges_metadata.stalk-shape|stalk-root|stalk-surface-abo: (LIST[STRING]) Attribute-value tuple defining the hyperedge for stalk shape, root, surface, and color attributes (e.g., [e, ?, k, s, w, w]).
edges_metadata.veil-type|veil-color|ring-number|ring-ty: (LIST[STRING]) Attribute-value tuple defining the hyperedge for veil, ring, and spore-print attributes (e.g., [p, w, o, p, r]).

datasets.name: NDC-classes
datasets.description: Nodes represent drug class labels. Each hyperedge represents a drug and contains the set of class labels applied to that drug. This is a temporal higher-order network dataset from the National Drug Code (NDC) Directory, maintained by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration under the Drug Listing Act of 1972. Timestamps are in days and represent when the drug was first marketed.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., NDC-classes).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., true).

nodes_metadata.class_label: (STRING) Drug class label assigned in the NDC Directory (e.g., radioactive diagnostic agent [epc]).

edges_metadata.drug_labels: (LIST[STRING]) Drug labels represented by this timestamped class-label set; multiple labels indicate duplicate raw records collapsed into the same temporal hyperedge (e.g., [(0002-1200)]).
edges_metadata.original_timestamp: (INT) Raw first-marketed timestamp from the Cornell source file, before shifting the temporal origin (e.g., 63474192000000).
edges_metadata.time: (INT) Normalized first-marketed timestamp, equal to original_timestamp minus the minimum raw timestamp in the dataset (e.g., 3547497600000).
edges_metadata.weight: (INT) Number of raw drug records collapsed into this timestamped hyperedge (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: NDC-substances
datasets.description: Nodes represent chemical substances. Each hyperedge represents a drug (identified by its NDC code) and contains the set of substances that make up that drug. This is a temporal higher-order network dataset from the National Drug Code (NDC) Directory, maintained by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration under the Drug Listing Act of 1972.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., NDC-substances).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., true).

nodes_metadata.substance_name: (STRING) Chemical or active ingredient name (e.g., florbetapir f-18).

edges_metadata.label: (LIST[STRING]) Drug product labels and NDC codes represented by the hyperedge (e.g., [amyvid (0002-1200)]).
edges_metadata.original_timestamp: (INT) Original drug listing timestamp before temporal normalization (e.g., 63474192000000).
edges_metadata.time: (INT) Normalized drug listing timestamp used by the temporal hypergraph (e.g., 3547497600000).
edges_metadata.weight: (INT) Number of drug product labels collapsed into this hyperedge (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: NTU2012
datasets.description: National Taiwan University 3D model dataset from Chen et al (Chen et al. 2003] Chen, D.-Y.; Tian, X.-P.; Shen, Y.-T.; and Ouhyoung, M. 2003. On Visual Similarity Based 3D Model Retrieval. In Computer Graph). The NTU dataset is composed of 2,012 3D shapes from 67 categories, including car, chair, chess, chip, clock, cup, door, frame, pen, plant leaf and so on. The graph structure is constructed using a probability graph based on node distance. See 'Hypergraph Neural Networks' (https://arxiv.org/pdf/1809.09401) for details on construction.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: amazon-reviews
datasets.description: Hypergraph built from Amazon product reviews (specifically, the collection of 5-core datasets). Nodes represent products and each hyperedge represents the set of products reviewed by a user. Nodes are labeled by product category.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: cat-edge-Brain
datasets.description: Nodes represent brain regions from an MRI scan. Hyperedges (pairs) represent connections between regions and are labeled with one of two categories: high fMRI correlation or similar activation patterns.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., cat-edge-Brain).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

edges_metadata.label: (STRING) Type of brain connectivity measure (e.g., Jaccard index similarity).

datasets.name: cat-edge-DAWN
datasets.description: In this dataset, nodes are drugs and hyperedges are combinations of drugs taken by a patient prior to an emergency room visit. Hyperedge categories indicate the most common patient disposition for that drug combination.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., cat-edge-DAWN).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.name: (STRING) Drug name with numeric code prefix (e.g., 182 CROMOLYN).

edges_metadata.label: (STRING) Patient disposition category for the drug-combination hyperedge (e.g., DISCHARGED HOME).
edges_metadata.label_id: (INT) Source disposition category identifier, from 1 to 10 (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: cat-edge-MAG-10
datasets.description: The MAG-10 network is a subset of the Microsoft Academic Graph where nodes are authors, hyperedges correspond to a publication from those authors, and the hyperedges are categorized by one of 10 computer science confereneces (e.g., 'WWW', 'KDD', 'ICML'). If the same set of authors published at more than one conference, we used the most common venue as the category and any cases where there is a tie were discarded. Papers with more than 25 authors were also omitted.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., cat-edge-MAG-10).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

edges_metadata.label: (STRING) Computer science conference or venue category assigned to the publication hyperedge (e.g., NeurIPS).

datasets.name: coauth-cs-CVPR
datasets.description: This dataset is a subset of the Microsoft Academic Graph in which nodes represent authors and hyperedges correspond to their publications in CVPR (a top-tier computer science conference). Papers with more than 25 authors were omitted. Note that this dataset is derived from cat-edge-MAG-10; consequently, some publications may be missing when the same set of authors published at multiple conferences in that source. In such cases, the most frequent venue of a given hyperedge was used as the interaction category, and ties were discarded.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: coauth-cs-multiplex
datasets.description: The MAG-10 multiplex hypergraph is a subset of the Microsoft Academic Graph where nodes are authors and hyperedges represent publications by those authors. The hypergraph is organized into layers, each corresponding to one of 10 computer science conferences (e.g., “WWW”, “KDD”, “ICML”). Within each layer, hyperedges capture publications at that specific venue. If the same set of authors published together at multiple conferences, we assigned the hyperedge to the layer of their most frequent venue, discarding cases where there was a tie. Papers with more than 25 authors were also omitted.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., coauth-cs-multiplex).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

edges_metadata.layer: (INT) Multiplex layer identifier corresponding to the publication venue (e.g., 4).

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., coauth-geology).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., true).

nodes_metadata.author: (STRING) Name of the Microsoft Academic Graph author represented by the node (e.g., B. Lesaffre).

edges_metadata.weight: (INT) Number of publications collapsed into this author-set/year hyperedge (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: contacts-primary-school
datasets.description: Data on face-to-face interactions were collected on Thursday, October 1st and Friday, October 2nd 2009. We recorded 77,602 contact events between 242 individuals (232 children and 10 teachers). In this setting, each child has on average 323 contacts per day with 47 other children, leading to an average daily interaction time of 176 minutes. Most contacts are brief, but long contacts are also observed. Contacts occur mostly within each class, and each child spends on average three times more time in contact with classmates than with children of other classes. Nodes represent individuals. Original data are pairwise contacts; hyperedges are constructed by promoting cliques of contacts into higher-order group interactions.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.id: (INT) Unique identifier for the student or teacher (e.g., 1426).

edges_metadata.original_time: (INT) Original timestamp of the contact event (seconds from start) (e.g., 31220).
edges_metadata.time: (INT) Normalized timestamp of the contact event (seconds from start) (e.g., 0).

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: foodwebanalysis
datasets.description: The data is collected from the Florida Bay food web. Bifan motifs in the original directed carbon-flow network are converted into submodular hyperedges. Available node labels/classes indicate whether a food-web compartment is a producer, a low-level consumer, or a high-level consumer.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., foodwebanalysis).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether repeated bifan hyperedges are stored as edge weights (e.g., true).

nodes_metadata.species: (STRING) Species, taxon, or food-web compartment name from Florida-bay-meta.csv (e.g., Thalassia).
nodes_metadata.label: (INT) Source trophic class code: 1=producer, 2=low-level consumer, 3=high-level consumer; blank when absent.
nodes_metadata.trophic_class: (STRING) Human-readable trophic class derived from label (e.g., producer).

edges_metadata.weight: (INT) Number of duplicate bifan rows collapsed into this normalized 4-species hyperedge.

datasets.name: tags-ask-ubuntu
datasets.description: Nodes represent tags. Hyperedges represent questions on askubuntu.com and contain the set of tags applied to each question. The timestamps are recorded at millisecond resolution but are normalized to start at 0.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., tags-ask-ubuntu).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

edges_metadata.label: (LIST[STRING]) Question identifier associated with the tag-set hyperedge (e.g., [1]).
edges_metadata.time: (INT) Question timestamp used by the temporal hypergraph; in the JSON it matches original_timestamp (e.g., 48942985907).

datasets.name: tags-math-sx
datasets.description: Nodes represent tags. Hyperedges represent questions on math.stackexchange.com and contain the set of tags applied to each question. The timestamps are recorded at millisecond resolution but are normalized to start at 0.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., tags-math-sx).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.tag: (STRING) Math Stack Exchange question tag/topic (e.g., elementary-set-theory).

edges_metadata.label: (LIST[STRING]) Question identifier associated with the tag-set hyperedge (e.g., [1]).
edges_metadata.time: (INT) Question timestamp used by the temporal hypergraph; in the JSON it matches original_timestamp (e.g., 9952566473).

datasets.name: tags-stack-overflow
datasets.description: Nodes represent tags. Hyperedges represent questions on stackoverflow.com and contain the set of tags applied to each question. The timestamps are recorded at millisecond resolution but are normalized to start at 0.

hypergraph_metadata.name: (STRING) Dataset name (e.g., tags-stack-overflow).
hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., true).

nodes_metadata.tag: (STRING) Stack Overflow tag represented by the node (e.g., c#).

edges_metadata.time: (INT) Normalized question timestamp used by the temporal hypergraph (e.g., 0).
edges_metadata.weight: (INT) Number of questions collapsed into this tag-set/time hyperedge (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: trivago-clicks
datasets.description: A hypergraph where nodes are accommodations (mostly hotels), and hyperedges are sets of accommodations for which a user performed the 'click-out' action during the same browsing session.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.label: (LIST[STRING]) Country where the accommodation is located (e.g., [Australia]).

datasets.name: walmart-trips
datasets.description: Hypergraph of Walmart shopping trips. Nodes represent products and each hyperedge represents a trip (basket) containing the set of co-purchased products, as released as part of a Kaggle competition. Products are assigned to one of ten broad departments on walmart.com (e.g., 'Clothing, Shoes, and Accessories'), which serve as node labels (with an additional 'Other' class).

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

datasets.name: whiskyfun
datasets.description: The data is collected from the Whiskyfun website's listings of distillery flavour profiles. Nodes are flavour descriptors, and hyperedges are distilleries containing the set of descriptors associated with them.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

edges_metadata.name: (STRING) Name of the whisky distillery (e.g., Aberfeldy Single Malt Scotch Whisky).

datasets.name: wikipedia-coedit
datasets.description: Co-edit network of Wikipedia articles, where nodes are users and hyperedges are articles edited by those users.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.user_id: (INT) Wikipedia user identifier represented by the node (e.g., 0).

edges_metadata.item_id: (INT) Wikipedia item or article identifier represented by the hyperedge (e.g., 0).

datasets.name: yelp_business_reviews
datasets.description: Yelp business reviews information where nodes correspond to businesses with meta-attributes such as business stars and location (latitude and longitude). Hyperedges correspond to users and contain the set of businesses reviewed by that user. The source listed here contains the raw data from which this hypergraph dataset is constructed. An alternative source would be 'https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/yelp-dataset/yelp-dataset'.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.lat: (STRING) Business location latitude. Stored as a string in JSON; can be converted to float (e.g., 40.0175444).
nodes_metadata.long: (STRING) Business location longitude. Stored as a string in JSON; can be converted to float (e.g., -105.2833481).
nodes_metadata.name: (STRING) Name of the Yelp business represented by the node (e.g., Oskar Blues Taproom).

edges_metadata.edge_id: (INT) Original hyperedge identifier for the reviewing user (e.g., 1).

datasets.name: zoo
datasets.description: Nodes correspond to animals in the Zoo database and use integer ids. Hyperedges correspond to shared attributes, i.e., each hyperedge contains the set of animals that share a given attribute. Node metadata records the animal name and type class.

hypergraph_metadata.weighted: (BOOL) Whether the hypergraph is weighted (e.g., false).

nodes_metadata.animal_name: (STRING) Animal name from the source row (e.g., aardvark).

edges_metadata.name: (STRING) Attribute name with value suffix (e.g., eggs-0=no eggs, eggs-1=lays eggs).
